moves.name: 5LP
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; SA2 cancel: +1/-5

moves.name: 5MP
moves.notes: True blockstring when chained into 5MP~MK even at max delay; SA2 cancel: +5/+4

moves.name: 5HP
moves.notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff and block

moves.name: 5LK
moves.notes: DR cancel is delayed until after the 3rd active frame; SA2 cancel: +2/-3

moves.name: 5MK
moves.notes: SA2 cancel: +9/+3

moves.name: 5HK
moves.notes: Super cancel is delayed until after active frames; SA2 cancel: +10/+4

moves.name: 2LP
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air); SA2 cancel: +1/-4

moves.name: 2MP
moves.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-10 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air); SA2 cancel: +8/+1

moves.name: 2HP
moves.notes: SA2 cancel: +10/+8

moves.name: 2LK
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK

moves.name: 2MK
moves.notes: Special/DR cancel (but not DI cancel) is delayed until after active frames; SA2 cancel: +7/+0

moves.name: j.LP
moves.notes: Fastest air-to-air; good hitbox priority

moves.name: j.MP
moves.notes: Puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; lower body projectile invuln 8-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: j.HP
moves.notes: Spikes airborne opponents on counter-hit/Punish Counter; has juggle potential (causes air reset when juggled after 236HK); lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs); hitbox extends farther beyond Ingrid's arm (visually deceptive); best downward reach of Ingrid's jump-ins for vertical approaches

moves.name: j.LK
moves.notes: Cross-up; good air-to-air for potential cross-ups (e.g. E. Honda Sumo Smash, M. Bison Devil Reverse)

moves.name: j.MK
moves.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 8-21f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: j.HK
moves.notes: Puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (can juggle into Target Combo extension); lower body projectile invuln 12-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs); best horizontal range of Ingrid's jump-ins

moves.name: 6MP
moves.notes: Very high active frame count for an overhead (up to +6 oH / 0 oB with meaty timing)

moves.name: 6HP
moves.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; cancelable into j.214P on hit or block (cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame); KD +10 after cancel into j.214LP; can lead to huge damage with Sun Crests and j.214PP hitconfirm; high pushback keeps it safe on block in most scenarios

moves.name: 4MK
moves.notes: 6f blockstring gap into 4MK~HP TC (up to 8f if delayed)

moves.name: 4HP
moves.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; slow but solid anti-air with an extremely rewarding Target Combo juggle follow-up; strong hitbox but no head or upper body invincibility (likely to trade if not timed early); cannot hit cross-up; very short horizontal range on grounded opponents; 7f blockstring gap into 4HP~HP TC (up to 11f if delayed)

moves.name: 5MP~MK
moves.notes: True blockstring even at max delay; SA2 cancel: +9/+1

moves.name: 4MK~HP
moves.notes: 6f blockstring gap between hits (up to 8f if delayed)

moves.name: 4HP~HP
moves.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents (opponent can crouch block even after stand blocking the initial 4HP); reduced KD Advantage if only the 2nd hit connects on a grounded opponent; 7f blockstring gap between hits (up to 11f if delayed); SA2 cancel: KD+53/+0

moves.name: j.HK~j.HK
moves.notes: Puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state

moves.name: LPLK
moves.notes: No true throw loop, but can dash to become +1 for a corner pseudo-throw loop (also in range for a 2-hit light confirm); can set up a LP Sun Shot that allows Ingrid to dash in with frame advantage while recovering quickly enough to anti-air if the opponent jumps out of the corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

moves.name: 4LPLK
moves.notes: Side switch; no true throw oki after corner side switch but can threaten with ranged normals, forward dash (+0), or Drive Rush normals; can set up a LP Sun Shot that allows Ingrid to dash in with frame advantage while recovering quickly enough to anti-air if the opponent jumps out of the corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

moves.name: HPHK
moves.notes: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

moves.name: 6HPHK
moves.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); see Drive Reversal.

moves.name: MPMK
moves.notes: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

moves.name: MPMK~66
moves.notes: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.375 (min/throw), 1.502 (min/block), 3.083 (max/final DR frame)

moves.name: MPMK or 66
moves.notes: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

moves.name: 236LP
moves.notes: Sends a slower projectile forward a short distance that pauses before continuing forward; useful approach tool that can be followed up with Drive Rush pressure from midrange; can only have 1 meterless Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with OD Sun Shot)

moves.name: 236MP
moves.notes: Sends a projectile forward a medium distance that pauses before continuing forward; can only have 1 meterless Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with OD Sun Shot)

moves.name: 236HP
moves.notes: Uncharged version has 2f extra whiff recovery if the projectile does not connect within the first 40 frames; sends a projectile upward that pauses before either coming back down (uncharged) or continuing straight forward mid-air (charged); charged version is useful for baiting a whiffed parry or punishing opponents trying to jump out of Ingrid's projectile zoning; uncharged version can be used to safely stock a Sun Crest at mid range if the opponent doesn't jump over it; can only have 1 meterless Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with OD Sun Shot)

moves.name: 236LPMP or 236LPHP
moves.notes: LP+MP gives the shorter/slower 236LP version (most useful for approaching from midrange); LP+MP gives the farther/faster 236MP version (more useful as a traditional zoning projectile); can only have 1 OD Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with another meterless Sun Shot); SA2 cancel: KD+61/+10

moves.name: 236MPHP
moves.notes: Uncharged version has 2f extra whiff recovery if the projectile does not connect within the first 35 frames; button combination determines projectile strength (LP+MP / LP+HP / MP+HP); sends a projectile upward that pauses before either coming back down (uncharged) or continuing straight forward mid-air (charged); charged version is useful for baiting a whiffed parry or punishing opponents trying to jump out of Ingrid's projectile zoning; uncharged version can be used to safely stock a Sun Crest at mid range if the opponent doesn't jump over it; can only have 1 OD Sun Shot on-screen at a time (but can be used in conjunction with another meterless Sun Shot); SA2 cancel: KD+61/+10

moves.name: 214LP (HOLD OK)
moves.notes: Builds 1 Sun Crest on frame 47; can hold the button to build up to 4 Sun Crests (stocks build slightly more quickly when held); held version builds Sun Crests on frames 42/88/134/182 (releasing the button early causes each stock level to be built 5f later); held version ends once 4 Sun Crests have been accumulated regardless of the initial stock count; button must be held for 23f (2-stock) / 85f (3-stock) / 131f (4-stock) respectively; if activated with 4 Sun Crests, Ingrid instead builds 3000 Drive gauge every 46 frames

moves.name: 214MP
moves.notes: With 0 Sun Crests, this version also comes out when input with HP

moves.name: 214HP (1 Stock)
moves.notes: Consumes 1 Sun Crest for more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to the 0-stock MP/HP version

moves.name: 214HP (2 Stock)
moves.notes: Consumes 2 Sun Crests for more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to the 1-stock HP version

moves.name: 214PP
moves.notes: Has the properties of the 1-stock HP version without spending any Sun Crests; SA2 cancel: KD+75/+11

moves.name: 214PP (1 Stock)
moves.notes: Has the properties of the 2-stock HP version but only costs 1 Sun Crest; has more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to 0-stock OD version; SA2 cancel: KD+83/+12

moves.name: 214PP (2 Stock)
moves.notes: Has properties greater than the 2-stock HP version or 1-stock OD version for 2 Sun Crests; SA2 cancel: KD+94/+16

moves.name: j.214LP
moves.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 17-33; builds 1 Sun Crest on frame 47; the animation lasts 48f regardless of when it was input during the jump (more total recovery if input late in the jump arc); landing recovery ranges from 8-30f depending on activation time; Ingrid is in a counter-hit state for the entire duration and a Forced Knockdown state during all airborne frames; if performed with 4 Sun Crests, Ingrid builds 3000 Drive gauge instead; can be canceled into from 6HP

moves.name: j.214MP
moves.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; with 0 Sun Crests, this version also comes out when input with HP; can be canceled into from 6HP; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (corner follow-ups possible if input low to the ground); Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

moves.name: j.214HP (1 Stock)
moves.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; consumes 1 Sun Crest for more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to 0-stock MP/HP version; can be canceled into from 6HP; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (corner follow-ups possible if input low to the ground); Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

moves.name: j.214HP (2 Stock)
moves.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; consumes 1 Sun Crest for more hits, extra damage, and longer range compared to 1-stock HP version; can be canceled into from 6HP; launches opponents upward into a limited juggle state; Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

moves.name: j.214PP
moves.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; has the properties of the 1-stock HP version without spending any Sun Crests; can be canceled into from 6HP; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (corner follow-ups possible if input low to the ground); Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

moves.name: j.214PP (1 Stock)
moves.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; has the properties of the 2-stock HP version but only costs 1 Sun Crest; can be canceled into from 6HP; launches opponents upward into a limited juggle state; Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

moves.name: j.214PP (2 Stock)
moves.notes: Can be used during Forward/Neutral jump only on frames 13-37; Ingrid will land before the projectile comes out if activated on jump frame 30-37; has properties greater than the 2-stock HP version or 1-stock OD version for 2 Sun Crests; can be canceled into from 6HP; puts opponents into a free juggle OTG bounce state; if the opponent Perfect Parries and Ingrid lands before all 4 hits connect, all remaining hits will fail to come out resulting in worse frame advantage; Ingrid is in a crouching state on the final landing recovery frame

moves.name: 236LK
moves.notes: Strong anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; deals 200 less damage on active frames 4-10

moves.name: 236MK
moves.notes: 2 hits; Low Crush 12-20f (not airborne during these frames); combos from light normals

moves.name: 236HK
moves.notes: 4 hits; Low Crush 25-35f (not airborne during these frames); on hit, Ingrid recovers mid-air on frame 77 with the opponent in a limited juggle state (can follow up with j.MP knockdown / j.HP reset / j.214P)

moves.name: 236KK
moves.notes: 3 hits with a 4th hit occurring on hit only; Low Crush 14-26f (not airborne); safe buffer option that becomes +1 vs. Burnout; SA2 cancel: +15/+5

moves.name: 22K
moves.notes: Counter that can absorb strikes (resulting in a counter-attack) or dissipate 1-hit non-Super projectiles; counter-attack is a strike knockdown that can be blocked if triggered by a low-recovery attack; absorbing a projectile results in 28f total recovery (often punishable if done from longer ranges); can be used to counter most strike-based Supers (useful option out of Drive Rush cancel on reaction to opponent's Super freeze)

moves.name: 22KK
moves.notes: Counter that can absorb strikes/throws (resulting in a counter-attack) or dissipate non-Super projectiles even with multiple hits; counter-attack is a strike knockdown that can be blocked if triggered by a safe jump (3f leniency on safe jump timing); after absorbing a projectile hit, Ingrid incurs up to 9f fully invincible recovery per hit (refreshing for each new hit absorbed) followed by 15f vulnerable recovery; can be used to counter most Supers (useful option out of Drive Rush cancel on reaction to opponent's Super freeze); Ingrid is +12 before opponent hits the ground but too far away for a juggle

moves.name: 6KKK
moves.notes: Teleports forward to the opponent's location and performs a kick that is safe on block; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (follow-ups possible on a corner airborne connect); can whiff in juggle routes where the opponent is flying backward too quickly

moves.name: 2KKK
moves.notes: Teleports above the opponent and comes down with an overhead strike that is plus on block; puts opponents into a limited juggle state (follow-ups possible in the corner); puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle OTG bounce state; can whiff over opponents that are walking forward

moves.name: 4KKK
moves.notes: Teleports away from the opponent; 22 vulnerable recovery frames after reappearing; Distance: 1.74

moves.name: 22PPP
moves.notes: Dark Ingrid only; tall 16-hit beam projectile that reaches fullscreen; expands forward during the first 4 active frames before reaching full range on the 5th active frame; most/all hits will connect even if opponent falls into the late active frames; chip damage deals 50% of its base damage rather than the usual 25%

moves.name: 214214K
moves.notes: Dark Ingrid only; immediately puts the opponent into Burnout on hit; cannot be canceled into from normals; hits twice on hit or once on block

moves.name: LP or LK
moves.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; 3 hits (all hit low despite the divekick-like animation); can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; Modern Controls: L

moves.name: MP or MK
moves.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; Modern Controls: M

moves.name: HP or HK
moves.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; Modern Controls: H

moves.name: HP+HK
moves.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; cannot steer the attack forward like the other Monoid attacks; knocks down on both grounded and airborne opponents; input has no kara-cancel leniency (must hit HP+HK on the exact same frame); Modern Controls: Sp

moves.name: j.X
moves.notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; gains a second hitbox on active frames 3-6 that can hit cross-up; Modern Controls: j.X

moves.name: 214236HP/HK
moves.notes: Slow 3-bar Super with low damage relative to its meter cost; unsafe on hit or block; on hit, Ingrid returns and high-fives the Monoid (can transform into Dark Ingrid using the LP~LP~4~LK~HK input before the high five, becoming much more unsafe on hit); Modern Controls: 214236H

moves.name: 236236K (0 Stock)
moves.notes: Can hold the button to spend up to 2 Sun Crests (+400 damage per crest but reduced oki); sends opponent ~1/2 screen away on hit; Ingrid's primary reversal while in Burnout; hitbox has a dead zone that can cause it to whiff through high airborne opponents; solid anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; 1st hit deals 400 damage and puts the opponent into a limited juggle state if it trades with a projectile (high float for easy hitconfirm)

moves.name: 236236 (1 Stock)
moves.notes: Hold the button for 30 frames to spend 1 Sun Crest for 400 extra damage and a farther knockdown; sends opponent ~2/3 screen away on hit; hitbox has a dead zone that can cause it to whiff through high airborne opponents; solid anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; 1st hit deals 400 damage and puts the opponent into a limited juggle state if it trades with a projectile (high float for easy hitconfirm)

moves.name: 236236 (2 Stock)
moves.notes: Hold the button for 60 frames to spend 2 Sun Crests for 800 extra damage and a farther knockdown; sends opponent a full screen away on hit; hitbox has a dead zone that can cause it to whiff through high airborne opponents; solid anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; 1st hit deals 400 damage and puts the opponent into a limited juggle state if it trades with a projectile (high float for easy hitconfirm)

moves.name: 214214P (0 Stock)
moves.notes: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 3 downward-hitting Super-priority projectiles (these projectiles disappear if Ingrid is hit); button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); can hold the button to spend up to 2 Sun Crests for additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f; gap between blocked projectiles is 8f / 7f (varies when switching between stand/crouch block, but always large enough to throw between projectiles); grounded opponents are put into a free juggle state by the 3rd projectile (1st/2nd keep the opponent grounded); airborne opponents are put into a free juggle state or maintain the existing juggle count; Ingrid cannot gain additional Super gauge while the projectiles are active or until the current combo ends

moves.name: 214214 (1 Stock)
moves.notes: Button must be held 26f during Super freeze; fires beacon into the air that spawns 4 sets of downward-hitting Super-priority projectiles (these projectiles disappear if Ingrid is hit); button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); consumes 1 Sun Crest in exchange for the additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f; gap between blocked projectiles is 8f / 7f / 10f (varies when switching between stand/crouch block, but always large enough to throw between projectiles); grounded opponents are put into a free juggle state by the 3rd projectile (1st/2nd/4th keep the opponent grounded); airborne opponents are put into a free juggle state or maintain the existing juggle count; Ingrid cannot gain additional Super gauge while the projectiles are active or until the current combo ends

moves.name: 214214 (2 Stock)
moves.notes: Button must be held 71f during Super freeze; fires beacon into the air that spawns 5 sets of downward-hitting Super-priority projectiles (these projectiles disappear if Ingrid is hit); button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); consumes 2 Sun Crests in exchange for the additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f; gap between blocked projectiles is 8f / 7f / 10f / 7f (varies when switching between stand/crouch block, but always large enough to throw between projectiles); grounded opponents are put into a free juggle state by the 3rd projectile (1st/2nd/4th keep the opponent grounded); airborne opponents are put into a free juggle state or maintain the existing juggle count; 5th hit puts opponent into an OTG bounce state; Ingrid cannot gain additional Super gauge while the projectiles are active or until the current combo ends

moves.name: 236236P
moves.notes: Projectile hitbox that targets the opponent's position (can be avoided if opponent is ascending, like jumping after the Super freeze); available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.3 Drive bars for Ingrid; very slow startup makes it a poor reversal

moves.name: 360+KK
moves.notes: Dark Ingrid only; command grab Super; opponent cannot jump after the freeze; can be comboed into on grounded opponents; cinematic time regenerates ~1.6 Drive bars for Ingrid
